records.ety: [Freq. of snuff, v.i.; akin to LG. snuffeln, G. schnüffeln, D. snuffeln, Dan. snövle. Cf. Sniffle.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Snuffled(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Snuffling (?).]

defs: To speak through the nose; to breathe through the nose when it is obstructed, so as to make a broken sound.
quotes: One clad in purple Eats, and recites some lamentable rhyme . . . Snuffling at nose, and croaking in his throat.

defs: The act of snuffing; a sound made by the air passing through the nose when obstructed.
quotes: This dread sovereign, Breath, in its passage, gave a snort or snuffle.

defs: An affected nasal twang; hence, cant; hypocrisy.
Obstruction of the nose by mucus; nasal catarrh of infants or children.
